title: Bounded Prefetch, Worker Ownership, Ordering, and Backpressure · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs bounded prefetch, worker ownership, ordering, and backpressure from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded cancellable Pokemon prefetch pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Define which next-view latency benefit justifies prefetch traffic and when no prefetch should occur.
Set worker, queue, request, byte, and time ceilings from fair-use and resource evidence rather than input size.
Assign which goroutine creates, sends, closes, drains, cancels, and waits for each channel and result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use blocked workers, tiny queues, cancellation, duplicate jobs, partial failures, and race or leak evidence under deterministic fixtures.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use blocked workers, tiny queues, cancellation, duplicate jobs, partial failures, and race or leak evidence under deterministic fixtures.
